One ROSTER-LEVEL absence surfaced during the audit (not this child's fault, but reportable per G3):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests") appears uncovered as a standalone deliverable. Each child (33d0 AC4, 2359 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, fe57) only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a criterion that produces the documented governing guardrail/policy artifact itself.

  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: add the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone) or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. The 7 cross-child interaction modes were each checked; ONE high-confidence finding (a cluster of interacting modes between 33d0 and sibling 2359 over the SAME files / WHICH-PATH-SURVIVES assumption). The symmetric counterpart is already filed on 2359; recorded here for this pairing's record from 33d0's side. Remaining modes read benign (detailed below).

---
FINDING — implicit shared state + conflicting assumptions + ordering violation + consumer impact (33d0 ↔ 2359)
- finding: 33d0 (collapse the dual outbound applier paths) and sibling 2359 (surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary) both rewrite the SAME outbound-apply region of the SAME files with no sequencing between them, and 33d0's "which path survives" decision is left explicitly open in a way that can invalidate 2359's central deliverable. 33d0 What: "Either delete the dead typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` (and its now-misleading tests), OR make it the single production path and route the batch dispatch through it." 2359 plumbs `links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied` counters + the `computed>0 && applied==0` canary into the BATCH path (`batch_dispatch.py` `update_one`, `applier.py` `RECON: batch_outcome` line) and cites "parity with the typed leaf's existing `links_applied`/no-op guard" as its reference model. If 33d0 chooses option 2 (route batch through the typed leaf) — or deletes the typed leaf whose `links_applied`/no-op guard 2359 references — 2359's instrumentation either lands on a non-surviving path or loses its cited reference model. Neither ticket declares a `depends_on`; both merely list the other as "Out", which partitions WORK but not the SHARED FILE STATE (batch_dispatch.py / applier.py) or the surviving-path assumption.
  - criteria: 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path; retired path deleted), 33d0 AC2 (combined multi-sub-op UPDATE through the production path); parent AC3/SC2 (33d0) vs parent AC4/SC3 (2359)
  - location: 33d0 What/Scope vs 2359 What/Scope; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py (_apply_outbound_update counters + no-op guard, L244-400: labels_applied list L245/L389, comments_applied int L278, links_applied int L325, no-op skip-guard L372-385); batch_dispatch.py (update_one link/comment/label dispatch, L490-561 — NO per-sub-op counter, label/comment/link failures print to stderr and silently continue); applier.py (RECON batch_outcome L816-821 emits only action/key/error); reconcile.py L75 ((outbound,update) → applier._apply_outbound_update) vs batch_dispatch.py L387-388 comment ("the production outbound dispatch routes through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf")
  - evidence: 33d0 file_impact = apply_outbound.py ("retire/consolidate typed leaf") + batch_dispatch.py ("production outbound path") + applier.py ("routing"); 2359 Scope.In = "outcome/telemetry plumbing in batch_dispatch.py / applier.py / reconcile.py"; repo: batch path (batch_dispatch.py update_one) has NO links_applied counter and logs sub-op failures only to stderr, while the typed leaf apply_outbound.py DOES carry links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied + the no-op guard 2359 re-derives; the codebase already contains BOTH a typed-leaf dispatch route (reconcile.py L75 / typed_dispatch.py L56) and a batch route (batch_dispatch.update_one) — the exact dual path 33d0 collapses
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 lands option 2 (batch routed through the typed leaf) → 2359's batch_dispatch counter/canary code is bypassed or removed; the silent-no-op canary that "would have caught the link drop" no longer guards the live path → parent AC4/SC3 silently regress to green-on-the-live-path, reinstating the bug-3f04 class on whichever path the counter is not carried into. (b) 33d0 deletes the typed leaf (option 1) → 2359 loses the `links_applied`/no-op-guard "parity" model it cites, and any test 2359 added against the typed leaf becomes a residual reference. (c) either order → same-region merge conflict in the ~40-line link/comment/label dispatch block.
  - impact: parent AC4/SC3 ("a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green") and parent AC3/SC2 (one production path) can both be checked off individually yet leave the surviving path UN-instrumented; avoidable same-region merge conflict; a residual reference to the deleted typed leaf in 2359.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — implicit shared state / conflicting assumptions / ordering violation / consumer impact
  - suggested_fix: sequence 33d0 BEFORE 2359 (collapse to ONE path first, then instrument the single survivor) and add an explicit `2359 depends_on 33d0`; OR state the canonical surviving production path in BOTH tickets so 2359 targets it unconditionally; OR fold the counter/canary requirement into 33d0's "single path" AC so the surviving applier carries telemetry by construction (and the labels-as-list vs count normalization is resolved on the one survivor).

---
Modes that read BENIGN (not filed):
- Scope overlap: 33d0 AC2 ("combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserts each sub-op took effect") vs 822a AC1 (bidirectional comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP) vs 2359 AC2 (no-op CANARY) are distinct intents (consolidation-correctness vs inbound↔outbound round-trip vs computed>0&&applied==0 invariant), exercising the same production path from different angles — complementary, not duplicative; 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→2359) consistently.
- Dependency gap (on inputs): 33d0 is pure code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so — unlike siblings 8937/59ef/822a which correctly declare depends_on fe57 — its lack of an input dep is appropriate. (The 33d0↔2359 sequencing gap is captured in the finding above under ordering.)
- Consumer impact on the contract test: 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 drive the differs→applier through "the production path"; once 33d0 collapses to one path their tests target the survivor by construction — benign provided 33d0 lands first, which the suggested sequencing already addresses.
- Residual references / conflicting assumptions with 8937/fe57/59ef: none — those stories key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface, not the outbound applier 33d0 rewrites.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set, not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not re-filed here.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 finding attributable to this child, and NO uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" → 33d0 AC1 (exactly one outbound applier path remains in production). FULLY.
- Parent AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" → 33d0 AC1 (retired path deleted; no dead, separately-tested applier code). FULLY.
- Parent SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0 AC1 + AC2 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — driven through the production path, each sub-op asserted to take effect). FULLY.
- Contributes to parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC3: no capability regresses, suite green) and to parent SC "new tests assert behavior/contract; zero change-detector" (33d0 AC4: combined test asserts effects/sub-op outcomes, never interaction/mock-call-counts).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT item (sub-op telemetry → story E/2359) is consistent with the roster:
- AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module" + AC "test validates fetcher snapshot vs schema" + AC "test validates each differ's reads vs schema" + SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on shape" → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake honesty).
- AC "one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures" → 8937 AC3 + fe57.
- AC "sub-op telemetry surfaced" + AC "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly (warn-first)" + SC "silent no-op fails canary" → 2359 (AC1/AC2/AC3).
- AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically" + AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded" + SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a.

CORRECTION TO PRIOR AUDITS ON THIS TICKET — the recurring "Parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) only PARTIALLY covered" finding is a FALSE POSITIVE and is WITHDRAWN for this pairing. The actual parent acceptance criterion reads verbatim: "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)." It (a) explicitly NAMES its owner — story B = sibling 8937 — and (b) is FULLY covered there: 8937's description states it "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests — a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 = "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests." Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a named consumer counts), this criterion is FULLY covered, not partial. The prior "AC6 PARTIALLY" finding was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text and predates 8937's explicit ownership; it is not re-filed. (The most recent G3 audit on sibling 2359 already withdrew the same false positive.)

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent "collapse to single production path", parent "retired path deleted", parent SC "production path exercised, not dead path" (all owned by THIS child); CONTRIBUTED — parent AC "suite green" + parent SC "zero change-detector"; COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/round-trip/telemetry/probe criteria (8937/fe57/59ef/2359/822a); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six closed; suite green". UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.

G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the repo.

RESOLVED since the prior G4 audit on this ticket (NOT re-filed): the earlier 33d0↔2359 "ordering violation / which-path-survives" finding rested on the absence of any sequencing edge. Sibling 2359 now declares `depends_on 33d0` (present in 2359 deps; restated in 2359's description "Sequencing: depends on story D (the single consolidated applier) so the counts come from one path"). 33d0 collapses to ONE production path FIRST, then 2359 instruments the single survivor — the same-region co-edit of batch_dispatch.py/applier.py is now sequenced rather than concurrent. The ordering-violation mode reads benign under current ticket state.

ONE high-confidence finding remains that the depends_on edge does NOT resolve — a representation conflict on the SHARED outbound-payload contract that 33d0 owns and 2359 builds its canary over:

FINDING — conflicting assumptions: 33d0 owns the surviving applier's sub-op payload contract, in which `labels_applied` is list-valued while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints; downstream sibling 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` count-canary assumes a uniform integer count, and 33d0 fixes no canonical label representation for the survivor.
- finding: 33d0 (What) "Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path" and its parity gate ("assert the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did") make 33d0 the owner of the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload. Repo-confirmed, that payload is NOT uniformly count-shaped: in the typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` (the path 33d0 may either delete or promote), `labels_applied` is a `list[str]` of decorated label strings (apply_outbound.py L245/L257/L260/L389) while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints (L325/L278). The existing typed-leaf tests that 33d0 retargets assert the EXACT list contents of `labels_applied` (test_applier_outbound_update.py L204 `== ["+tag-alpha","+tag-beta"]`, L233 `== ["-stale-tag"]`). Sibling 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then surfaces `labels_applied` in the SAME count-and-invariant frame as links/comments and defines a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` canary "parity with the inbound applier's existing links_applied" (an int). 33d0 nowhere states the canonical label representation for the single surviving payload, so the survivor 2359 instruments is handed an ambiguous label signal: keep the list (2359's `applied == 0` predicate is ill-typed over a list — `[]` ≠ `0`, a non-empty list is truthy) or flip to an int (breaks the exact-list asserts 33d0 just retargeted). Either way a shared contract is left unowned at the seam between THIS child (owns the survivor's payload) and 2359 (consumes it for the canary).
  - criteria: 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path; retired path deleted), 33d0 AC2 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — asserting each sub-op took effect), 33d0 parity gate (surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did); cross-checked against 2359 AC1 (surface labels_applied) + 2359 AC2/AC5 (computed>0 && applied==0 count canary); parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path" (33d0) vs parent AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly" (2359)
  - location: 33d0 What/Scope (retarget typed-leaf tests; parity gate over the surviving payload); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py L245 (`labels_applied: list[str] = []`), L257/L260 (`.append(f"+{label}")`/`f"-{label}"`), L389 (`"labels_applied": labels_applied`) vs L278/L325 (`comments_applied`/`links_applied` int); batch_dispatch.py update_one L480-559 (the production batch path applies labels/comments/links but tracks NO per-sub-op counter today — the survivor 33d0 must reconcile against the typed-leaf shape); apply_inbound.py inbound `links_applied` int (the parity model 2359 cites); tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_outbound_update.py L204/L233 (exact-list asserts on labels_applied) — the tests 33d0 retargets
  - evidence: repo confirms the surviving-applier candidate payload carries `labels_applied` as list[str] and links/comments_applied as ints; the existing tests 33d0 retargets assert exact label-list contents (not a count); the production batch path 33d0 may keep has NO per-sub-op counter at all (L480-559), so 33d0's parity gate must MINT the label signal's representation for the survivor; 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then layers a count-based `computed>0 && applied==0` invariant on that very key citing an int parity model
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 promotes/retargets keeping `labels_applied` as a list to satisfy its retargeted exact-list tests → 2359's `applied == 0` canary over labels mis-fires or never fires (empty list `[]` ≠ `0`; non-empty list truthy), so the silent-no-op guarantee silently fails for the labels sub-op specifically even while links/comments are covered. (b) 33d0 (or 2359) normalizes `labels_applied` to an int → the exact-list assertions at test_applier_outbound_update.py L204/L233 that 33d0 retargeted break → parent "no capability regresses; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4) regresses. (c) 33d0 leaves the representation unstated → the surviving-applier payload contract (33d0) and the canary (2359) disagree on which field/shape the invariant reads.
  - impact: 33d0's parity gate and retargeted tests cannot be checked off consistently with 2359's count-canary unless 33d0 fixes the surviving payload's label representation; a silent risk to parent SC "a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" (holds for links/comments but possibly not labels) and to parent "suite green" (33d0 AC4) if the list key is flipped.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — conflicting assumptions (shared outbound-payload representation between 33d0's surviving applier and 2359's count-canary)
  - suggested_fix: in 33d0, fix the surviving consolidated applier's per-sub-op payload representation explicitly — keep the existing list-valued `labels_applied` AND expose a derived count (e.g. `labels_applied_count` / `len(labels_applied)`) so the exact-list tests survive while a count exists for the invariant — and state that one representation in 33d0's parity/AC so 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` predicate is well-typed over labels by construction across the single production path.

SECONDARY (lower-standing, recorded under implicit-shared-state; the depends_on largely tames it): 33d0 and 2359 co-edit the SAME ~80-line link/comment/label dispatch region of batch_dispatch.py update_one (L480-559) and the applier.py RECON batch_outcome line (L816-821). 2359's depends_on 33d0 sequences the work so 33d0 lands first; the residual is only that 33d0's side carries no reciprocal note that 2359 will re-touch the same region to add counters/canary, and 33d0 should leave the surviving dispatch loop structured so per-sub-op counts can be threaded in without re-architecting it. This reads benign given the existing dependency edge; folded into the suggested_fix above (have 33d0's single-survivor AC carry the label-signal representation so 2359 only adds the invariant, not a reshape).

Modes that read BENIGN (not filed): ordering violation — RESOLVED via 2359 depends_on 33d0. Dependency gap (on inputs) — 33d0 is pure outbound-code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so its lack of a depends_on on fe57/8937 is appropriate (unlike 822a/59ef which correctly declare depends_on fe57). Scope overlap — 33d0 AC2 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect) vs 822a AC1 (bidirectional inbound↔outbound round-trip) vs 2359 AC2 (computed>0&&applied==0 canary) are distinct intents over the same production path; 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→2359) consistently. Consumer impact — once 33d0 collapses to one path, 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 (which drive "the production path") target the survivor by construction; benign given 33d0 lands first per the dependency graph. Residual references — 33d0 explicitly deletes the dead typed leaf AND its now-misleading tests (33d0 AC1 + the parity-before-delete gate), so it does not strand references; siblings 8937/fe57/59ef/822a key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface or drive the production path, not the deleted typed leaf.

The roster-level parent-AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) matter is a G3 coverage question — and is in any case owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not addressed here.

comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, independently repo-corroborated this pass.

REPO VERIFICATION (this pass):
- Dual path confirmed: typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` is registered at typed_dispatch.py L56 ((outbound,update)) and reconcile.py L75, while the PRODUCTION outbound dispatch routes through the batch path `batch_dispatch.py update_one` (its own comment L387-388: "the production outbound dispatch routes through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf _apply_outbound_update"). This is exactly the drift 33d0 collapses.
- Representation asymmetry confirmed: in the typed leaf, `labels_applied` is `list[str]` (apply_outbound.py L245 `labels_applied: list[str] = []`, L257/L260 append `f"+{name}"`/`f"-{name}"`, L389 `"labels_applied": labels_applied`) while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints (L325/L278), and inbound `links_applied` is also an int (apply_inbound.py L469) — the int "parity" model 2359 cites.
- The existing no-op skip-guard at apply_outbound.py L372-378 uses TRUTHINESS (`not labels_applied`), NOT `== 0`.
- The PRODUCTION batch path carries NO per-sub-op counter at all: update_one (batch_dispatch.py L501-559) applies comments/links/labels but never tallies them, and applier.py L816-821 RECON batch_outcome emits only action/key/error. So when 33d0 collapses onto the surviving (production/batch) path, its parity gate MUST MINT the per-sub-op result representation for the survivor.

ONE high-confidence finding remains (a representation conflict at the 33d0→2359 seam that the declared `2359 depends_on 33d0` edge sequences but does NOT resolve). This is the symmetric counterpart of the finding already filed on 2359; recorded here for this pairing's record from 33d0's side.

FINDING — conflicting assumptions: 33d0 owns the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload (it retargets the typed-leaf tests at the survivor and its parity gate asserts the survivor applies every sub-op the retired path did), in which `labels_applied` is list-valued while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints; downstream sibling 2359 (depends_on 33d0) layers a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` count-canary over those very keys assuming a uniform integer count, and 33d0 fixes no canonical label representation for the single survivor.
- finding: 33d0 What ("Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path") + its parity gate ("assert the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did — fields, comments, labels, links, parent") make 33d0 the owner of the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload. Because the production batch path has no counters today, 33d0 must establish that payload's shape for the survivor. The typed-leaf candidate shape it would carry over is NOT uniformly count-shaped (`labels_applied` list[str]; links/comments ints). Sibling 2359 then surfaces `labels_applied` in the SAME count-and-invariant frame as links/comments and defines a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` canary citing int parity. 33d0 nowhere states the canonical label representation, so the survivor 2359 instruments is handed an ambiguous label signal: keep the list (2359's `applied == 0` predicate is ill-typed over a list — `[]` ≠ `0`, a non-empty list is truthy) or flip to an int (breaks any exact-list label assertion 33d0 just retargeted). Either way a shared contract is left unowned at the seam between THIS child (owns the survivor's payload) and 2359 (consumes it for the canary). The depends_on edge sequences the work (33d0 lands first) but does not by itself decide the label representation.
  - criteria: 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path; retired path deleted), 33d0 AC2 (behavioral parity — surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did, before deletion), 33d0 AC3 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — asserting each sub-op took effect); cross-checked against 2359 AC1 (surface labels_applied) + 2359 AC2/AC5 (computed>0 && applied==0 count canary); parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path" (33d0) vs parent AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly" (2359)
  - location: 33d0 What/Scope (retarget typed-leaf tests; parity gate over the surviving payload); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py L245 (`labels_applied: list[str] = []`), L257/L260 (append `+`/`-` label strings), L389 (`"labels_applied": labels_applied`) vs L278/L325 (`comments_applied`/`links_applied` int), L372-378 no-op guard (truthiness `not labels_applied`, not `== 0`); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py L469 (inbound `links_applied: int` — the parity model 2359 cites); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py update_one L501-559 (production path applies comments/links/labels but tracks NO per-sub-op counter); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py L816-821 (RECON batch_outcome emits only action/key/error); typed_dispatch.py L56 + reconcile.py L75 (typed-leaf route) vs batch_dispatch.py L387-388 (production routes through the legacy batch path)
  - evidence: repo confirms the surviving-applier candidate payload carries `labels_applied` as list[str] and links/comments_applied as ints; the production batch path carries NO per-sub-op counter, so 33d0's parity gate must mint the label representation for the survivor; 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then layers a count-based `computed>0 && applied==0` invariant on that very key citing an int parity model; the no-op guard 33d0 may carry over uses truthiness, not a `== 0` count
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 lands the survivor keeping `labels_applied` as a list to satisfy its retargeted exact-list tests → 2359's `applied == 0` canary over labels mis-fires or never fires (`[]` ≠ `0`; non-empty list truthy) → the silent-no-op guarantee silently fails for the labels sub-op specifically even while links/comments are covered → reinstates a labels-shaped instance of the bug-3f04 class. (b) 33d0 (or 2359) normalizes `labels_applied` to an int → any exact-list label assertion 33d0 retargeted breaks → parent "no capability regresses; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4) regresses. (c) 33d0 leaves the representation unstated → the surviving-applier payload contract (33d0) and the canary (2359) disagree on which field/shape the invariant reads.
  - impact: 33d0's parity gate and retargeted tests cannot be checked off consistently with 2359's count-canary unless 33d0 fixes the surviving payload's label representation; a silent risk to parent SC "a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" (holds for links/comments but possibly not labels) and to parent "suite green" (33d0 AC4) if the list key is flipped.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — conflicting assumptions (shared outbound-payload label representation between 33d0's surviving applier and 2359's count-canary)
  - suggested_fix: in 33d0, fix the surviving consolidated applier's per-sub-op result representation explicitly — keep the existing list-valued `labels_applied` AND expose a derived count (e.g. `labels_applied_count` / `len(labels_applied)`) so the exact-list tests survive while an integer count exists for the invariant — and state that one representation in 33d0's parity/AC so 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` predicate is well-typed over labels by construction across the single production path. Also structure the surviving dispatch loop so 2359 can thread per-sub-op counts in without re-architecting it.

SECONDARY (implicit shared state — recorded, not separately filed; the depends_on largely tames it): 33d0 and 2359 co-edit the SAME comment/link/label dispatch region of batch_dispatch.py update_one (L501-559) and the applier.py RECON batch_outcome line (L816-821). `2359 depends_on 33d0` sequences the work so 33d0 lands first; the residual is only that 33d0 carries no reciprocal note that 2359 will re-touch this region to add counters/canary, and 33d0 should leave the surviving dispatch loop structured so per-sub-op counts thread in cleanly — folded into the suggested_fix above.

Modes that read BENIGN (not filed): ordering violation — RESOLVED via `2359 depends_on 33d0` (collapse to one path first, then instrument the single survivor). Dependency gap (on inputs) — 33d0 is pure outbound-code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so unlike 822a/59ef (which correctly declare depends_on fe57) its lack of an input dep is appropriate. Scope overlap — 33d0 AC3 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect) vs 822a AC1 (bidirectional inbound↔outbound round-trip) vs 2359 AC2 (computed>0&&applied==0 canary) are distinct intents over the same production path; 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→story E/2359) consistently. Consumer impact — once 33d0 collapses to one path, 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 (which drive "the production path") target the survivor by construction; benign given 33d0 lands first per the dependency graph. Residual references — 33d0 explicitly deletes the dead typed leaf AND its now-misleading tests (33d0 AC1 + the parity-before-delete gate), so it does not strand references; siblings 8937/fe57/59ef key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface, not the deleted typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail matter is a G3 coverage question (and is owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

All five acceptance criteria are met by the implementation. Evidence grounded in the repository:

**AC1 — Exactly one outbound applier path remains; retired path deleted.**
`_apply_outbound_update` in `apply_outbound.py` (L125–161) is now a thin delegator: it calls `_mutation_to_batch_dict(mutation)` + `update_one(batch, client)` — mirroring `_apply_outbound_delete`. The ~240-line parallel implementation is gone; `_index_existing_links`, `_route_status_via_draft5`, and the `labels_applied`/`links_applied`/`comments_applied` counter variables are absent from the file. The old dead-path test files `test_applier_outbound_update.py` and `test_applier_outbound_links.py` are deleted from `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/`. The typed leaf remains as a DISTINCT dispatch binding (required by the registry) but carries no parallel application logic.

**AC2 — Behavioral parity verified before retired path deleted.**
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_outbound_update_combined.py` (lines 162–190) contains `test_typed_leaf_delegates_to_update_one`, which asserts `_apply_outbound_update` produces the same client effects (update_issue, add_label, add_comment, set_relationship) as calling `update_one` directly, and that the leaf no longer returns its own sub-op counters. The module docstring explicitly identifies this file as the parity gate for the collapse.

**AC3 — Single test drives fields + comments + labels + links + parent through the production path in one UPDATE.**
`test_combined_all_subops_take_effect_through_update_one` (lines 124–154) issues one `batch.update_one(mutation, client)` call carrying `fields={"summary": ..., "status": ..., "parent": ...}`, `labels=[add + remove]`, `comments=[...]`, and `links=[add]`, then asserts each sub-op took effect: scalar update (update_issue), set_parent, add_label, remove_label, add_comment, set_relationship.

**AC4 — No capability regresses.**
The dispatch tables still bind `(outbound, update)` → `_apply_outbound_update` (typed_dispatch.py L56, reconcile.py L75, applier.py L104/L339). The production `_apply_batch` path continues to route outbound updates through `update_one` (applier.py L476). Additional retargeted tests in `test_update_one_field_filter.py`, `test_update_one_parent_routing.py`, `test_link_sync.py`, and conflict tests all exercise the surviving production path.

**AC5 — Combined test asserts effects (sub-op outcomes), not interaction/mock-call-counts.**
The combined test uses a `_RecordingClient` and asserts what operations *took effect* and with what arguments (e.g., `client.set_parent_calls == [("DIG-1", "EPIC-9")]`, `client.add_comment_calls == [("DIG-1", "status note")]`). These are outcome assertions, not arbitrary call-count contracts.

## Why

Outbound link sync broke because production routes outbound updates through the **batch** applier (`batch_dispatch.update_one` + `_mutation_to_batch_dict`), while the link/comment/label features and their tests lived in the **typed-leaf** applier `apply_outbound._apply_outbound_update` — which production does not execute. Capabilities had to be re-added to the batch path one bug at a time (parent 8b25, comments 87e4, links 3f04). Two parallel outbound appliers guarantee the next capability will drift the same way.

## What

- Collapse outbound application to **one** path. Either delete the dead typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` (and its now-misleading tests), or make it the single production path and route the batch dispatch through it — chosen so there is no separately-tested parallel that production skips.
- Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path.
- Add **one** test that drives a multi-sub-op outbound UPDATE — scalar fields + comments + labels + links + parent together — through the production path in a single mutation (the sub-ops were only ever tested in isolation, in separate files).
- **Safety (rollout):** verify behavioral parity BEFORE deleting the retired path — assert the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did (fields, comments, labels, links, parent), so the deletion cannot silently drop a capability.

## Scope

- In: applier consolidation in `apply_outbound.py` / `batch_dispatch.py` / `applier.py`; the combined multi-sub-op test; the parity check.
- Out: sub-op telemetry (story E).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Exactly one outbound applier path remains in production; the retired path is deleted (no dead, separately-tested applier code).
- [ ] Behavioral parity is verified — the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did — before the retired path is deleted.
- [ ] A single test drives fields + comments + labels + links + parent through the production path in one UPDATE and asserts each sub-op took effect.
- [ ] No capability regresses; the full reconciler suite is green.
- [ ] The combined test asserts effects (sub-op outcomes), never interaction/mock-call-counts.
